executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict continues at MAJOR intensity with 830 incidents in 24h. The most significant escalation is IRGC's ballistic missile volley against the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier — the first known Iranian attack on a US aircraft carrier. UK has authorized US strikes from British bases, Trump indicates the war could last 4-5 weeks, and the conflict has expanded to target central Iranian cities (Yazd, Isfahan) while Bahrain and UAE face direct Iranian strikes. Three British nationals were killed in Dubai, and Hezbollah's senior political leader was assassinated in Beirut.
whats_new: IRGC launched ballistic missile volley at USS Abraham Lincoln carrier — first known Iranian attack on a US aircraft carrier, representing direct escalation to US naval assets
Israeli-US strikes expanded to central Iranian cities Yazd and Isfahan — significant geographic escalation of strikes inside Iran
Hezbollah political leader Mohammed Raad killed in Israeli airstrike on Beirut — targeting of senior Hezbollah leadership
British air base in Cyprus struck by suspected drone — expansion of attacks beyond Iraq-Syria theater to British assets
Three British nationals killed in Dubai by Iranian suicide drones — direct Western casualties in Gulf strikes
US military briefly acknowledged (then deleted) Iranian strike on Haifa oil refinery — potential confirmation of direct Iranian attack on Israeli civilian energy infrastructure
IAEA emergency meeting scheduled for March 2 to discuss US-Israeli strikes on Iran — international nuclear watchdog responding to conflict
confirmed_events: CENTCOM confirms 3 US troops killed, 5 wounded in Iranian retaliatory strikes on bases in Iraq/Syria region — first direct US casualties
Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei confirmed dead in joint US-Israeli strike on Tehran — confirmed by multiple sources including IRGC statements
Hezbollah formally entered the war with precision missile/drone strikes on Haifa missile defense site — in retaliation for Khamenei's killing
Iran attacked offshore facility near Salman Port, Bahrain — missile debris killed one worker, injured two, marking first direct hit on Bahraini territory
UK authorized US to use British bases for limited strikes on Iranian missile storage depots and launchers — direct US-UK military action against Iran
Amazon Web Services data centre in UAE struck by Iranian missiles/drones causing fire and power outage — confirmed by multiple sources
Oil price surged to $80/barrel, Asian stocks dropped — market reaction to Strait of Hormuz attacks and tanker strikes
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims it shot down an American MQ-9 Reaper drone in Isfahan airspace — single-source claim from IRGC, not independently confirmed
Report from Iran: IDF and US Army attacking in Kerman and Shiraz — reports from Iranian sources, limited verification
Israeli strikes killed more than 20 people in Lebanon — multiple Lebanese reports, casualty figures pending verification
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi sent message to UN Security Council regarding 'revolution leader's martyrdom' — diplomatic communication but details unverified

people_travel: Cathay Pacific suspended all Middle East operations — major commercial aviation disruption
British nationals advised to shelter in Dubai as city hit by Iranian suicide drones — travel advisory升级
Multiple Gulf airports and airspace affected by ongoing missile and drone activity
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz attacked by US-Israeli forces — critical global oil chokepoint disrupted
Amazon Web Services data centre in UAE knocked out — regional cloud infrastructure impacted
Bahrain Salman Port targeted — Gulf maritime logistics disrupted
Shipping routes through Persian Gulf under threat — insurers and carriers likely rerouting
market_macro: Oil prices surged to $80/barrel — highest level in recent months
Asian stock markets dropped significantly — regional market volatility
Energy infrastructure attacks (Haifa refinery, UAE facilities) adding supply concerns

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict is likely to intensify over the next 24-72h. With Trump indicating 4-5 week timeline and IRGC now targeting US carriers, we can expect continued exchanges of strikes. The UK authorization for US strikes from British bases and the expansion of US-Israeli operations to central Iranian cities (Yazd, Isfahan) signal the conflict will deepen. Hezbollah's formal entry and Iran's attacks on Gulf allies (UAE, Bahrain) indicate the war is broadening geographically.
escalation_risks: IRGC ballistic missile attack on USS Abraham Lincoln could provoke US carrier group retaliation — risk of major naval engagement
Israeli strikes on Beirut killing Hezbollah leadership may trigger massive Hezbollah response against Israeli cities
Iranian attacks on Gulf states (UAE, Bahrain) could draw those countries more directly into conflict
Central Iranian city strikes (Yazd, Isfahan) may trigger broader Iranian retaliation against Gulf and regional targets
MQ-9 drone downing (if confirmed) represents further escalation in US-Iran direct engagement
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears unlikely in the near term. Iran's Security Council has denied WSJ reports of talks with US. Trump has signaled openness to talks with 'new leadership' but conditions for Iranian surrender (weapons handover + immunity) appear non-negotiable. The killing of Khamenei and senior Iranian military leadership has removed potential negotiating counterparts. International pressure via IAEA emergency meeting may create diplomatic off-ramp, but both sides appear committed to sustained military campaign.
